relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Paul refuses performance-wisdom as the container for Divine mystery.
He centers only Jesus Messiah, crucified.
Weakness, fear, and trembling become honest ground.
Spirit and power demonstrate what polished persuasion cannot.
Trust rests in Divine power, not human wisdom.
There is wisdom among the mature, but not the fading wisdom of the age.
Hidden Divine wisdom waits beneath the surface of the age.
The rulers did not know the glory they crucified.
The prepared things exceed eye, ear, and heart.
Spirit unveils by searching even Divine depths.
Only spirit knows inner depths; only Divine Spirit knows Divine things.
We receive Spirit from the Divine to know what grace gives.
Spirit-taught speech interprets spiritual things spiritually.
The soul-level self cannot receive what only Spirit discerns.
The spiritual one discerns from another depth.
The mind of Messiah becomes shared perception.

contemplative_note: 1 Corinthians 2 protects true wisdom from ego-wisdom. Spirit searches depths and teaches the community to perceive through the mind of Messiah.
